content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.
Now I understand the structure. I need to add a follow-up discount when `previousQuotes >= 1`. The discount is 15% of the base premium.

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_03-50-02_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Wait - looking at the test expectation: `expected 115 to be 100`. The current implementation returned 115 which means first insurance was applied (100 + 10 + 5 = 115). The test description says "100 base + 10 first ins - 15 followup + 5 fee = 100". So both first insurance AND follow-up discount apply. Let me re-check.

The expected value: 100 + 10 - 15 + 5 = 100. So first insurance still applies! I changed that incorrectly. Let me revert.

content.text:
All 17 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added `FOLLOWUP_DISCOUNT_RATE = 0.15` constant and applied a follow-up discount (15% of base premium) when `customer.previousQuotes >= 1`.
**Result**: All tests now pass (17 passing, 24 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal addition mirroring the existing loyalty discount pattern - a threshold check that subtracts a percentage of the base from the total. Calculation: 100 base + 10 first insurance - 15 follow-up discount + 5 fee = 100 G.
